brief_justification: Paper A is an experimental quantum-networking architecture that dynamically routes and allocates polarisation-entangled photon pairs to users via a reconfigurable optical add-drop multiplexer, whereas paper B is a stochastic-PDE regularity proof showing transport noise regularizes the 3D Navier-Stokes equations to yield global smooth solutions with high probability. There is no common governing process or role-for-role correspondence: hardware resource allocation over fibre versus a probabilistic blow-up-suppression argument share neither machinery nor structure.
